name: Pina Colada Pie
recipeInstructions:
----------------------------------FILLING----------------------------------
1 1/2 pints Pineapple sorbet, softened
1 large pineapple, thinly sliced
1 cup Sweetened cream of Coconut
4 teaspoons Dark Rum
Fresh Mint sprigs
1 tablespoon Imitation coconut Extract
2 pints Vanilla frozen yogurt
1. Prepare the crust: Preheat oven to 350 degrees F (175 degrees C). Line a 10 inch glass pie dish with foil, overlapping sides. In a food processor, finely chop the roasted macadamia nuts, shredded coconut, and melted butter. Add brown sugar and process until moist crumbs form. Press mixture into the bottom and up the sides (but not the rim) of the prepared pie dish. Freeze for 10 minutes.
2. Bake the crust: Place the pie in the oven and bake until golden brown, about 15 minutes. Remove from the oven and let it cool for 20 minutes.
3. Prepare the filling: Spread the softened pineapple sorbet evenly over the baked crust. In a heavy saucepan, boil the cream of coconut over medium-high heat until reduced to 3/4 cup, stirring frequently, about 7 minutes. Pour into a large bowl and mix in rum, extract, and remaining coconut. Cool slightly.
4. Combine ingredients: Add frozen yogurt to the coconut mixture and fold until blended. Let it cool for about 1 hour, stirring occasionally.
5. Assemble the pie: Spoon the filling over the sorbet in the pie dish, mounding slightly. Cover with foil and freeze for at least 2 hours, or overnight. When ready to serve, turn the pie out onto a platter, peel off the foil, and turn it right side up. Arrange pineapple slices decoratively on top and sprinkle with toasted coconut. Garnish with mint leaves. Enjoy!
